content.problemStatement: Merge accounts that share common emails and return each user’s name followed by their sorted, unique emails.
content.inputFormat: A 2D array of strings where each sub-array represents an account with the first element as the account name and the rest as email addresses.
content.outputFormat: A 2D array of strings where each sub-array represents a merged account with the first element as the account name and the rest as sorted unique email addresses.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use a Disjoint Set (Union-Find) data structure to group accounts with common email addresses.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N * M * log(M)) where N is the number of accounts and M is the maximum number of email addresses in an account.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N * M) for storing the Disjoint Set and the email addresses.
